title: Recognizing a century of broadcasting excellence from WOWO and celebrating the radio station's 100th anniversary.
plain_summary: This bill celebrates the 100th birthday of WOWO, a radio station in Fort Wayne, Indiana. The station started in 1925 and became an important conservative talk radio station in the Midwest. For 100 years, WOWO has helped educate families and bring communities together. The House of Representatives wants to recognize the radio station for doing an excellent job of broadcasting to people for a whole century.
last_action_text: Sponsor introductory remarks on measure. (CR H1344)

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution recognizes one hundred years of broadcasting excellence from WOWO radio station and celebrates the radio station's one hundredth anniversary.

sections.title: Background Information
sections.text: WOWO radio station started in nineteen twenty-five as one of the early radio stations in the United States. It grew to become one of the strongest radio stations in the Midwest after joining the CBS Radio Network in nineteen twenty-seven. WOWO was the first radio station ever to broadcast a basketball game, showing its Indiana spirit. By the nineteen fifties, WOWO became a popular station for news, sports, and entertainment. It brought Fort Wayne into the rock and roll era and was home to beloved radio personalities like Bob Sievers. In the nineteen nineties, WOWO changed into the conservative talk radio station we know today. WOWO has been a steady source of well-expressed conservative values. It features trusted local commentators like Pat Miller, Charly Butcher, and Kayla Blakeslee, along with nationally broadcast figures like Rush Limbaugh and Sean Hannity. WOWO has inspired and informed countless families in the United States about important issues. It has helped shape its community into a place with strong conservative principles and common sense. WOWO has been lovingly called The Voice of a Thousand Main Streets. This nickname honors its deep connections to the heartland of the United States and its role in bringing news, conversation, and connection to its listeners.

sections.title: House Resolution
sections.text: The House of Representatives celebrates WOWO's one hundredth anniversary on the air. The House recognizes WOWO's record of excellent broadcasting, which has strengthened communities and educated countless families on the most important issues. The House supports WOWO's efforts to continue informing and inspiring generations to come.
